steps.title: Identify the specific measurement needed from the table
steps.content: The question asks for “the average amount contributed…by each individual employed in wholesale trade.” This specifies two requirements: the industry (Wholesale trade) and the type of value (per-employee average contribution, not total revenue or employee count).

steps.title: Locate the correct row and column, then read the value
steps.content: Find the Wholesale trade row and the “Average contribution per employee by industry” column; their intersection shows $183,790. The 141,510 dollar option uses a different industry's average (Tribal economic activity), while the other distractors confuse the data type by giving employee counts instead of contribution figures. That matches the Wholesale trade value.
